findings.title: CTA links to /diagnostic which does not exist
findings.principle: Norman — error prevention; links must resolve
findings.evidence: Line 219: <Link href="/diagnostic"> but no src/app/diagnostic/ route exists. No src/app/audit-tool/ either. Click produces a 404.
findings.fix: Create the /diagnostic route or point the CTA to an existing page (e.g., /audit-tool, Cal.com booking link, or homepage #cta).

findings.title: CTA box uses rounded-lg, violating NULL//TRACE zero-radius rule
findings.principle: Design system consistency — NULL//TRACE aesthetic requires border-radius: 0
findings.evidence: Line 198: className="mt-16 rounded-lg p-8 text-center". The .urbit-landing zero-radius override does not apply because the about page does not have the urbit-landing class.
findings.fix: Remove rounded-lg. Either add the urbit-landing class to the page wrapper or use rounded-none explicitly.

findings.title: Gold accent used for headline and CTA border despite design system ban
findings.principle: Design system — sienna gold is banned per CLAUDE.md; only --brand-gold on logo 'ads' is exempt
findings.evidence: Line 73: For operators. and line 201: border: '1px solid var(--color-gold)'. CLAUDE.md states: 'Sienna gold is banned. Do not use --color-gold anywhere.'
findings.fix: Replace gold headline accent with --text-primary or --color-dark. Replace CTA border with var(--border-subtle) or var(--color-dark). The logo 'ads' span on line 51 is the only valid gold usage (--brand-gold).

findings.title: No photo, no team, no proof of operator identity
findings.principle: Norman — discoverability; 'built by an operator' claim needs evidence
findings.evidence: The page is 100% text. No headshot of Blake, no photo of a facility, no portfolio details. The operator-credibility claim is unsupported visually.
findings.fix: Add a photo of Blake (ideally at a facility). Mention the number of facilities or years operating. Even one concrete detail ('I run [X] facilities in [state]') adds proof.

findings.title: Sign-off has redundant role lines
findings.evidence: Lines 185-190: 'Founder, StorageAds.com' followed by 'Storage operator & founder'. Both say 'founder'. Redundant.
findings.fix: Collapse to a single line: 'Founder & operator, StorageAds.com' or just 'Founder, StorageAds'.

findings.title: No visual break between story sections — wall of text
findings.principle: Krug — scanning; visual hierarchy must guide the eye
findings.evidence: Three story sections separated only by 1px hairline dividers. On mobile, this reads as one unbroken essay. No pull quotes, no callout boxes, no visual anchors between dividers.
findings.fix: Add a bold pull quote or a single-stat callout between sections to break the wall and reward scanners.

findings.title: Only one CTA at the very bottom of a long scroll
findings.principle: Fitts's law — action must be reachable when intent peaks
findings.evidence: The single CTA is after ~800 words of story. An operator who is convinced at paragraph 3 has to scroll to the bottom to act.
findings.fix: Add a soft inline CTA or sticky bottom bar after the first divider section ('Want to see your numbers? Get a free audit').

findings.title: 'cost per move-in' used as a phrase in body copy
findings.principle: Copy voice guide — 'cost-per-move-in' is banned as a hero phrase in customer-facing copy
findings.evidence: Line 127: 'My cost per move-in dropped.' The copy-voice.md says: 'Cost-per-move-in (as a hero phrase)' is on the do-not-use list.
findings.fix: Rewrite to operator language: 'What I paid per new tenant dropped.' or 'Each move-in cost me less.'

findings.title: Closing paragraph has awkward syntax
findings.principle: Copy voice — one idea per sentence, concrete over abstract
findings.evidence: Line 166-168: 'It's the system I built for my own facilities, by someone who signs the same checks you do.' The 'by someone' clause dangles — the subject of 'it's the system I built' is already first-person, then switches to third-person 'someone'.
findings.fix: Rewrite: 'This isn't a marketing agency. I built this for my own facilities. I sign the same checks you do.'

findings.title: Meta description uses 'cost-per-move-in' phrasing indirectly and title says 'StorageAds' not 'storageads'
findings.evidence: Line 6: title says 'About StorageAds' with camel case. The brand per CLAUDE.md is lowercase 'storageads'. Minor but inconsistent with the nav rendering on line 50-51.
findings.fix: Use 'storageads' (lowercase) in metadata title, or establish that title-case is acceptable in meta tags specifically.

findings.title: CTA says 'free facility audit' but copy says 'free diagnostic' — terminology mismatch
findings.principle: Lean UX — consistent naming for the conversion artifact
findings.evidence: Line 216: 'Get a free diagnostic' but line 219: 'Get your free facility audit'. Also href is /diagnostic. Three terms for one thing: diagnostic, audit, facility audit.
findings.fix: Pick one term and use it everywhere. The frame doc calls it 'audit'. Use 'free audit' consistently.

findings.title: Nav breadcrumb style ('/ About') looks unfinished
findings.principle: Visual hierarchy — breadcrumbs need intentional styling
findings.evidence: Line 57: A plain '/ About' span in tertiary color next to the logo. No structured breadcrumb pattern — just a text slash.
findings.fix: Use a proper separator (a thin vertical bar or a styled slash) or drop the breadcrumb entirely — the back arrow already signals navigation.

findings.title: No skip-to-content link for keyboard/screen reader users
findings.evidence: The page has a sticky header but no skip link to jump to the article content.
findings.fix: Add a visually hidden skip link as the first focusable element: Skip to content.

findings.title: Back arrow has no accessible label
findings.evidence: Line 37-41: The ArrowLeft icon link has no aria-label or visible text. Screen readers announce it as an empty link.
findings.fix: Add aria-label='Back to home' to the Link component.

strategic_moves: Fix the dead /diagnostic CTA immediately — it is the only conversion path and it 404s.
Add a photo of Blake and one concrete operator detail (number of facilities, location) to substantiate the 'built by an operator' claim.
Strip all --color-gold usage except the logo 'ads' span to align with the design system ban — use contrast-based (dark/light) accent instead.
